response: The global food system faces unprecedented stress from population growth, climate change, resource limits, and waste. By 2050 the world’s population is projected to reach 9.7 billion, requiring about a 60% increase in food production, while climate change and water scarcity reduce yields in many major farming regions. Agriculture also contributes roughly 25% of greenhouse gas emissions, and soil degradation has reduced productivity on 33% of the world’s arable land; additionally, about one-third of produced food is lost or wasted between farm and fork. Proposed responses include precision agriculture (AI/sensors), vertical farming, alternative proteins, drought-resistant genetic modification, and regenerative farming, alongside policy reforms—though the key challenge is scaling these solutions affordably for the world’s poorest.
